routing.reasoning: Constant ~2.0mm wall over a 15×40mm planar footprint (thinnest extent 2.5mm ≈ gauge, planar aspect 6:1) → a flat sheet, not a printed/cut solid. Route to sheet-metal / stamping: flat laser/punch blank, 110mm cut length. Powder-bed/MJF here is a prototyping fallback, not the production route.

notes: DFM note: processes injection_molding are costed but flagged NOT DFM-ready as-modeled (the engine reports ERROR-level blockers — these parts were modeled for 3D printing, so molding/casting lack draft). Their cost shows the tooling economics *if* the part is redesigned for that process. Set strict_dfm=True to exclude them entirely.

Geometric routing: this part reads as a 'sheet_panel' → sheet_metal. Constant ~2.0mm wall over a 15×40mm planar footprint (thinnest extent 2.5mm ≈ gauge, planar aspect 6:1) → a flat sheet, not a printed/cut solid. Route to sheet-metal / stamping: flat laser/punch blank, 110mm cut length. Powder-bed/MJF here is a prototyping fallback, not the production route.
Note: the cost-cheapest make-as-is pick at low qty is fdm, but the geometry-recommended process is sheet_metal — both are costed above; pick on intent, not just the marginal $.
Absolute cost is ±40–60% (cycle-time/tooling defaults). The crossover quantity and make-vs-buy direction are robust to it because they depend on the fixed-vs-variable split, driven by your rates.

assumptions.source: perishable tooling/consumables as a fraction of CNC machine cost [assumption, not shop-validated]

assumptions.name: material_class

decision.note: Make by fdm (PLA) — $30.0/unit at qty 1, the cheapest make-as-is option and your low-volume pick. fdm stays cheapest up to ~2489 units; above ~2489, injection_molding is cheaper ($3.38/unit at qty 10000). Note: injection_molding requires design-for-molding — the part currently FAILS draft DFM (8 sidewall faces (100.0% of sidewall area) below 1.0° draft for injection_molding.); the tooling cost shown is 'if redesigned for molding', not a current-capability quote.
